column3: =-  Peek

column1: Here we compare the capabilities of various systems, how they relate to each other and how they fit into one or more systems.
column3: Peek tries to stay out of your way, invisible, fusing with your OS.
column4: Darc focuses heavily on AI agents and security.

column3: Manage your personal structured data produced by extensions and web pages.
Sync data entities with other devices.
Navigate through a timeline of your browsing history.
Write CSS and JS scripts to modify web pages.
Window manager for the web: Arrange web views. Use keyboard shortcuts to bring them up or hide them.
column4: Monitor how web pages & agents use various resources and the permissions they asked for plus the ones they act on.

column3: Workbench for the web: Bring tools into view when needed (uncluttered work bench). Ability to chain tools (command chaining).
column4: Toggle extensions on a per web page basis.

column3: Execute commands provided by extensions using a "command executer" extension.
column4: Lock everything down by default to make sure every part of the browser, including all extensions, are cryptographically verified and only audited components are used.
Run docker containers right in the browser to host web applications and then use those apps. Load directly from a Github repo.
Explicitely hibernate a web view, or force it to load on start up.
Use an agent to native through the web.

column4: Open web pages in various tabs or windows.

column3: Though Peek escapes the traditional browser window and provides a more tighter OS-level integration.
column4: Darc is more similar to the browser in this regard, because it is based on IWAs, a web browser technology.

column3: Search the web without going to the search engine's web page first, keeping the web search tool close.
column4: Manage your opened web pages in spaces (similar to Zen & Arc).

column3: Make groups of web pages. This heavily varies between browsers.
column4: Organise your tabs in a vertical tab side bar + pinning.
Put web pages in a specific container.
